name: Ansotano Aragonese (Ansó valley)

ancestors.notes: Ansotano is a Western High-Aragonese valley variety inheriting the whole Aragonese grapheme→IPA system; it declares only the word-final ⟨r⟩ deletion delta and documents lexical/morphological distinctiveness in notes.
Hispanic Vulgar Latin, inherited via Aragonese (an).
Basque/Aquitanian substrate: the Ansó valley was Basque-speaking into the early medieval period, inherited via Aragonese.

notes: Ansotano — the Aragonese of the Ansó valley (Valle de Ansó, Huesca), a Western High-Aragonese variety, one of the most conservative and best-preserved traditional valleys (documented since Saroïhandy and Kuhn 1935). PHONOLOGICALLY-EXPRESSED DELTA modelled here: word-final ⟨r⟩ is NOT pronounced in Ansó (deleted in the infinitive and in nouns), whereas it is retained in neighbouring Fago and in Cheso — 'la -r final no se pronuncia en Ansó pero sí en Fago' (es.wikipedia 'Aragonés ansotano', secondary, after Barcos 2007). Modelled as a positional delta ⟨r⟩ word_final → [∅]; the base word-initial [r], intervocalic [ɾ] and non-final coda [ɾ] are preserved. Ansotano otherwise keeps the full Western Aragonese profile inherited from an: F- preserved, /θ/ distinción, /ʎ/ for ⟨ll⟩, ⟨ch⟩ = [tʃ]. FEATURES NOT PHONEME-MAPPABLE (documented, not modelled — Aragonese spelling is phonemic and these are lexical/morphological): retention of intervocalic -d- in words like 'odir' (oír); article o/a/os/as; conservative verb morphology. Diphthongisation Ĕ/Ŏ → ie/ue is inherited (Várvaro, AFA XLVI-XLVII, p. 250 discusses the Ansó documents). TIER RATIONALE: skeleton — the single robustly source-attested phoneme-level delta (final-r deletion) is modelled, but most of Ansotano's distinctiveness is lexical/morphological and the primary phonological monographs (Kuhn 1935; Barcos 2007; Benítez Marco 2001) were not directly obtainable, so the phonological description here rests on a secondary summary. PROVENANCE HONESTY: per-valley detail from the Spanish-Wikipedia dialect article (secondary, citing Kuhn/Barcos/Benítez Marco); Várvaro (AFA) read directly.

sources.author: Kuhn, A.
sources.title: Der hocharagonesische Dialekt
sources.publisher: Revue de Linguistique Romane XI, pp. 1-312
sources.notes: The classic High-Aragonese dialect monograph, foundational for Ansó and Hecho. Cited via the secondary Spanish-Wikipedia dialect article; primary not directly obtainable.

sources.author: Barcos, M. A.
sources.title: El aragonés ansotano: estudio lingüístico de Ansó y Fago
sources.publisher: Gara d'Edizions / Institución Fernando el Católico
sources.notes: Cited via the secondary Spanish-Wikipedia dialect article; primary not directly obtainable. Source of the Ansó vs Fago final-r contrast.

sources.author: Várvaro, A.
sources.title: De la escritura al habla: la diptongación de O breve tónica en el Alto Aragón
sources.notes: Read directly. Medieval Ŏ-diphthongisation geography including the Ansó documents (p. 250).
